subject: Physics
title: Supernovae: Exploding Stars
description: A short lesson to help you apply Supernovae: Exploding Stars.

hook.text: A supernova outshines an entire galaxy for weeks. It's the most violent explosion in the universe.

buildup.text: Massive stars burn through their fuel fast. When the core runs out, gravity wins and the star collapses.

discovery.text: The collapse rebounds off the dense core, creating a shock wave that blows the star apart at 30,000 km/s.

twist.text: Supernovae forge elements heavier than iron—gold, platinum, uranium. Everything heavy on Earth was born in a dying star.

climax.text: In 1054 AD, Chinese astronomers recorded a supernova visible in daylight for 23 days. Its remnant is the Crab Nebula.

punchline.text: Stars die violently. Their debris becomes planets, moons, and you.

quiz.question: Where are elements heavier than iron created?
options: In the cores of living stars
